current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: Run the stabilized gated configuration in proxy at K=32 with AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 to test whether the improved gated family can replace the current JumpReLU K=32 Pareto point. full
Run a proxy gated K=32 stabilization pass with AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 and EXPANSION_FACTOR=8 to test for a better low-K cost-quality tradeoff.

Run the first real proxy comparison for `group_topk` at K=128 using the current strongest gated training regime, but without the unsupported `GROUP_TOPK_SIZE` env override that caused the earlier crashes.

recent_round_summaries.hypothesis: The stabilized gated regime has now improved both K=128 and K=64, so the next clean Pareto probe is to test whether that same family extends to K=32 and can displace the older JumpReLU low-K point. A gated K=32 proxy run under the same validated settings will show whether the current best family can dominate more of the frontier rather than remaining a high-K-only solution.

Now that gated holds the full frontier at K=128, K=64, and K=32, the next informative move is to test whether the low-K regime is over-capacitated rather than quality-limited. Reducing gated expansion_factor from 16 to 8 at K=32 may preserve most of the newly won quality while improving cost signals and possibly sharpening feature competition enough to strengthen the Pareto tradeoff.

The last round showed the gated low-K capacity cut was a real quality regression, and the search has now gone several rounds without advancing an incubating alternative family. The least-bad informative move is to get a real proxy read on the already-implemented `group_topk` family at K=128 under the strongest current training regime, removing the unsupported `GROUP_TOPK_SIZE` override that caused the earlier policy crashes. This will finally test whether local winner-take-all competition can beat other non-gated support selectors on anchor-quality rather than being discarded for an execution-layer mismatch.

topk.design_hypothesis: Five non-improving rounds on gated-specific tweaks suggest the current search direction is locally exhausted, so the next meaningful move is to revisit a fundamentally different sparse selection mechanism. A clean top-k mainline comparison at K=128 using the current strongest training regime may show whether gated-specific routing is the bottleneck rather than the optimizer or regularization settings.
next_steps: If top-k proxy FVU improves materially over its earlier result and is competitive with gated, promote it to full and begin top-k stabilization from this stronger anchor.
If top-k remains clearly worse even under the stronger regime, conclude that a different architectural direction is needed and retire top-k from near-term focus.
If top-k is competitive on quality but notably better on runtime or stability, keep it alive as an alternate mainline family for subsequent cost-quality tradeoff work.

group_topk.design_hypothesis: The last round showed the gated low-K capacity cut was a real quality regression, and the search has now gone several rounds without advancing an incubating alternative family. The least-bad informative move is to get a real proxy read on the already-implemented `group_topk` family at K=128 under the strongest current training regime, removing the unsupported `GROUP_TOPK_SIZE` override that caused the earlier policy crashes. This will finally test whether local winner-take-all competition can beat other non-gated support selectors on anchor-quality rather than being discarded for an execution-layer mismatch.
next_steps: If `group_topk` is competitive with gated at K=128, keep the family alive and follow with a code-round that softens or enriches the grouped selection mechanism inside `sparsify/`, since group-size sweeps are not exposed by the execution layer.

If `group_topk` is clearly worse on quality under a normal run, retire hard local grouping and move to a more expressive multi-branch or factorized encoder family instead of more support-ranking variants.
If `group_topk` is mediocre on FVU but materially better on runtime or memory, evaluate it as a cost-quality tradeoff family before discarding it.

recent_performance_findings: round 3: suspected performance regression for jumprelu (tps=16601.650675, baseline_ratio=0.200000, termination=throughput_too_low)
next_move_guidance: If `group_topk` is competitive with gated at K=128, keep the family alive and follow with a code-round that softens or enriches the grouped selection mechanism inside `sparsify/`, since group-size sweeps are not exposed by the execution layer.

If `group_topk` is clearly worse on quality under a normal run, retire hard local grouping and move to a more expressive multi-branch or factorized encoder family instead of more support-ranking variants.
If `group_topk` is mediocre on FVU but materially better on runtime or memory, evaluate it as a cost-quality tradeoff family before discarding it.
